title: Similarity-based Learning: k-NN Classification Process
description: A visual representation of how a k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN) algorithm classifies a new, unlabeled data point based on its similarity (proximity) to existing labeled data points in the feature space.
elements: Training Data Points (Labeled: Class A, Class B)
relationships: Training data populates the feature space
Distance metric calculates the similarity between the query point and all training points
k value determines how many of the most similar (nearest) points to consider
Majority vote mechanism counts the labels of the k-nearest neighbors
The most frequent class label among the neighbors is assigned to the query point
